content.claim: Approximately 270 schools nationally are currently endangered by low enrollment (under 80 students, averaging ~10 pupils per class), with consolidation/merger likely necessary; this closure wave is occurring in both rural areas and urban areas including Budapest, and policy focuses on protecting small-settlement demand rather than mandating closures.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: The 270-school figure is stated as a 'first-wave' estimate but methodology for calculation not detailed
Current demand status of at-risk schools not quantified; 'protection as long as there is demand' is a policy intention, not a binding constraint
No projection of how many of these 270 schools are primary (exposed to declining cohorts) vs. secondary (potentially affected only later)
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
